title: XRAY report on version 1.13.3
body:
Hi all, we're considering introducing skopeo as a dependency in a project and we've scanned last version in XRAY
Found vulns are:

- CVE-2018-20225 (in a pip whl package (and pip doesn't seem installed), no upstream fix, disputed) [HIGH]
- CVE-2023-39318, CVE-2023-39319 and CVE-2023-39323 [MEDIUM] due to go version 1.20.7
- CVE-2023-3978 [MEDIUM] due to component golang.org/x/net version 0.11.0

My questions are the following:
- Is the pip whl package needed?
- Is there a new release in preparation that will solve these issues (if possible), and when will it be released?
- I cannot find a release schedule of the project, is there any?

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

- I don’t have your scanner output, I don’t know what you see. AFAICT `pip` is only referenced in build scripts that are not involved in actually building Skopeo, but I don’t know what you see.
- Similarly, I see no reference to Go `1.20.7` anywhere, but I don’t know what you see. If you are building Skopeo from source, you can of course use a newer toolchain. The container images are built using packages from Fedora stable updates.
- The x/net vulnerability is in a subpackage which is not even included in the Skopeo binary.

As a matter of general principle, we want to make releases which fix real vulnerabilities, and to not bother to satisfy every possible false positive of every possible scanner product.

Skopeo tends to make releases very close to the Podman release schedule, as it is determined by that project. That’s once in a few months = several times a year.

Hi, thank you for your answer.

However, it appears that indeed skopeo 1.13.3 in quay container has been built using Go 1.20.7.

skopeo 1.13.3 has been released on Aug 25, while Fedora 38 Go version 1.20.8 has been released under updates on Sept 07, according to https://packages.fedoraproject.org/pkgs/golang/golang-bin/fedora-38-updates.html
Moreover also using string command on skopeo binary shows the string "go1.20.7".

The update to 1.20.8 in Fedora has been classified as security according to metadata and thus AFAIK the CVE reported in my previous comment are not false positive, even if they may or may not really apply to skopeo.
This is why I opened the issue, to understand if they are been evaluated and deemed not applicable for skopeo (or acknowledged and scheduled to be fixed in next release), not to bother you with random scanner false positive results.
Regards

Thanks for the details. It’s a reasonable question to ask — but given the analysis already performed, we are not going to do _extra_ work to make a new build, causing many Skopeo users to spend time upgrading, just to satisfy a scanner’s false positive.
